suburban.label: First generation, all-steel Carryall
suburban.notes: The first Chevrolet Suburban generation used an all-steel wagon body on a commercial chassis.

suburban.label: AK Series / Art Deco, postwar continuation
suburban.notes: The 1946 model resumed the prewar body after the 1943-1945 civilian model-year gap.

suburban.label: Advance Design
suburban.notes: Chevrolet's Advance Design truck body supplied the Suburban's third-generation base. Advance Design also appeared as the 1955 First Series, handled in the transition band.

suburban.label: Advance Design First Series / Task Force Second Series
suburban.notes: Both designs existed in model year 1955, so the transition is one nonoverlapping mixed band.

suburban.label: Task Force
suburban.notes: Unambiguous full model years of the Task Force Suburban.

suburban.label: First-generation C/K chassis
suburban.notes: This body follows Chevrolet's first C/K truck generation.

suburban.label: Action Line
suburban.notes: The three-side-door body is unique to this Action Line era.

suburban.label: Rounded Line C/K / Square Body
suburban.notes: Rounded Line is the GM-era name; Square Body is the dominant enthusiast alias.

suburban.label: Rounded Line R/V / Square Body continuation
suburban.notes: GM renamed the continuing C/K architecture R/V while the new GMT400 pickups entered production.

suburban.label: GMT400 family, GMT410
suburban.notes: GMT410 is the Chevrolet Suburban program within the GMT400 family.

suburban.label: GMT800 family, GMT830
suburban.notes: GMT830 is the long-wheelbase Suburban and Yukon XL derivative of GMT800.

suburban.label: GMT900 / GMT930 / GMT931
suburban.notes: GM's proprietary upfitter documentation identifies 931 as Chevrolet Suburban within the GMT900/GMT930 family.

suburban.label: K2XX / GMTK2YC
suburban.notes: K2YC is the Chevrolet long-wheelbase SUV program.

suburban.label: T1XX / GMTT1YC refresh
suburban.notes: The 2025 exterior and interior refresh remains on the T1XX/GMT1YC program.

tahoe.label: GMT400 family, GMT420
tahoe.notes: The Tahoe name replaced the full-size Blazer name for 1995.

tahoe.label: GMT420 carryover / GMT820 launch
tahoe.notes: Model year 2000 is genuinely mixed: Tahoe Limited and Z71 retained GMT420 while the redesigned Tahoe launched on GMT820.

tahoe.label: GMT800 family, GMT820
tahoe.notes: GMT820 is the standard-wheelbase Tahoe and Yukon derivative of GMT800.

tahoe.label: GMT900 / GMT920 / GMT921
tahoe.notes: GM's proprietary upfitter documentation identifies 921 as Chevrolet Tahoe within the GMT900/GMT920 family.

tahoe.label: K2XX / GMTK2UC
tahoe.notes: K2UC is the Chevrolet standard-wheelbase SUV program.

tahoe.label: T1XX / GMTT1UC refresh
tahoe.notes: The 2025 exterior and interior refresh remains on the T1XX/GMT1UC program.

avalanche.label: GMT800 family, GMT805
avalanche.notes: GMT805 is the Chevrolet Avalanche program. Related GMT806 belongs to the Cadillac Escalade EXT and is not a Chevrolet model code.

avalanche.label: GMT900 family, GMT941
avalanche.notes: Chevrolet's Avalanche program is GMT941 within the GMT940 derivative family.

blazer.label: First-generation K5 Blazer
blazer.notes: Short-wheelbase removable-roof SUV based on the Action Line C/K truck.

blazer.label: Second-generation K5, Rounded Line
blazer.notes: The continuing K5 was renamed V-Series after the C/K pickup changeover.

blazer.label: Full-size Blazer, GMT415
blazer.notes: The full-size two-door Blazer became Tahoe for model year 1995.

blazer.label: S/T Blazer, GMT330 family
blazer.notes: After 1995, the unqualified Blazer name identified the compact and midsize S/T SUV.

blazer.label: C1XX crossover, C1UC
blazer.notes: The revived Blazer is a unibody crossover unrelated to the K5 and S/T chassis.

blazer.label: C1XX crossover refresh, C1UC
blazer.notes: The 2023 appearance refresh remains on C1XX.

camaro.label: Fifth generation, Zeta
camaro.notes: The revived Camaro used GM's Zeta rear-wheel-drive architecture.

camaro.label: Sixth generation, Alpha
camaro.notes: Alpha coupe program A1BC and convertible program A1AC.

silverado.label: First generation, GMT800
silverado.notes: Silverado became the pickup nameplate rather than a trim.

silverado.label: GMT800 Classic / GMT900 launch
silverado.notes: Both old-body Classic and redesigned GMT900 light-duty trucks were sold as 2007 models.

silverado.label: Fourth generation, T1XX
silverado.notes: T1XX light-duty Silverado, including the 2022 midcycle refresh.

silverado-hd.label: GMT880 Classic / GMT910 launch
silverado-hd.notes: Transition model year with old and redesigned HD trucks.

silverado-hd.label: Second generation HD, GMT910
silverado-hd.notes: GMT910 HD continued through model year 2014 after light-duty GMT900 ended.

ck-series.label: Rounded Line / Square Body C/K
ck-series.notes: Light-duty C/K name ended after 1987 as the body continued under R/V for selected models.

ck-series.label: GMT400 Classic and C3500HD carryover
ck-series.notes: The catalog retains C/K through 2002 because heavy-duty and chassis-cab GMT400 derivatives outlived light-duty replacement.

colorado.label: Second generation, GMT31XX
colorado.notes: The global second generation returned to the U.S. for model year 2015.

colorado.label: Third generation, 31XX-2
colorado.notes: Chevrolet's chief engineer confirmed the updated 31XX-2 architecture.

express.label: GMT610
express.notes: The 2003 chassis and front-body revision was redesignated GMT610 and continues through 2026.

trailblazer.label: GMT360 / GMT370
trailblazer.notes: Standard-wheelbase models are GMT360; EXT models are GMT370.

trailblazer.label: VSS-F crossover
trailblazer.notes: U.S. return as a unibody subcompact crossover.

trailblazer.label: VSS-F crossover refresh
trailblazer.notes: The 2024 styling and cabin refresh remains on VSS-F.

ssr.label: GMT368
ssr.notes: Retractable-hardtop pickup on the dedicated GMT368 derivative.

s10-blazer.label: First-generation S/T Blazer
s10-blazer.notes: GMT325 and GMT330 are body/program variants within the first S/T SUV generation; unqualified Blazer replaces the S-10 Blazer catalog identity for 1995.

g-series-van.label: Third-generation Chevrolet van
g-series-van.notes: Long-running third generation, replaced by Express after 1996.

traverse-limited.label: C1XX / C1YC carryover
traverse-limited.notes: The Limited identity preserved the prior-generation C1YC vehicle alongside the redesigned 2024 Traverse.

trax.label: First U.S. generation, Gamma II / G1XX

malibu.label: First generation, A-body
malibu.notes: Malibu began as an upper Chevelle series.

malibu.label: Sixth generation, Epsilon
malibu.notes: First Epsilon Malibu; the 2008 carryover is separately cataloged as Malibu Classic.

malibu.label: Eighth generation, Epsilon II
malibu.notes: Short eighth generation on Epsilon II.

caprice.label: First generation, B-body
caprice.notes: Caprice began as a 1965 Impala subseries.

caprice.label: Fourth generation, B-body
caprice.notes: Final North American rear-wheel-drive Caprice generation.

caprice-ppv.label: GM Zeta, Holden WM/WN Caprice PPV era
caprice-ppv.notes: Official General Motors guides establish the continuous 2011-2017 U.S. Caprice police-package run. The Holden WM/WN source identifies the exported U.S. PPV and its GM Zeta lineage. The 9C3 Detective package ends after 2013, but the shared base-era band continues through the 2017 9C1 program.

nova.label: First generation, X-body
nova.notes: Nova began as a Chevy II series.

nova.label: Third generation, X-body
nova.notes: Nova became the dominant model name during this generation.

nova.label: Fifth generation, NUMMI E80
nova.notes: Front-wheel-drive Toyota-derived Nova built at NUMMI.

lumina.label: First generation, W-body
lumina.notes: First Lumina sedan and coupe generation.

sonic.label: Gamma II / T300
sonic.notes: The U.S. T300 was sold as Sonic.

bolt-ev.label: BEV2 / G2KCZ refresh
bolt-ev.notes: 2022 appearance refresh on the same BEV2 program.

blazer-ev.label: BEV3 / Ultium
blazer-ev.notes: Electric Blazer is architecturally separate from the C1XX gasoline Blazer.

equinox-ev.label: BEV3 / Ultium
equinox-ev.notes: Electric Equinox is architecturally separate from the gasoline Equinox.

silverado-ev.label: BT1XX / Ultium
silverado-ev.notes: Dedicated electric truck architecture, not a T1XX powertrain variant.

brightdrop-400.label: BV1 / Ultium commercial van
brightdrop-400.notes: Chevrolet-badged BrightDrop 400 begins with model year 2025; the official 2026 GM Fleet Guide confirms the second model year.

brightdrop-600.label: BV1 / Ultium commercial van
brightdrop-600.notes: Chevrolet-badged BrightDrop 600 begins with model year 2025; the official 2026 GM Fleet Guide confirms the second model year.

low-cab-forward.label: Isuzu N-Series-derived LCF
low-cab-forward.notes: Current Chevrolet cab-over family sourced from Isuzu N-Series architecture.

p-series-step-van.label: First-generation Dubl-Duti
p-series-step-van.notes: Chevrolet introduced the first Dubl-Duti multi-stop delivery van in 1940 on its pre-AK light-truck chassis.

p-series-step-van.label: AK-Series Dubl-Duti
p-series-step-van.notes: The Dubl-Duti was restyled for Chevrolet's AK truck line in 1941; civilian production stopped after 1942.

p-series-step-van.label: AK-Series Dubl-Duti, postwar continuation
p-series-step-van.notes: The AK-based Dubl-Duti resumed after the war and continued through 1948 despite the passenger-cab truck changeover.

p-series-step-van.label: Advance Design Dubl-Duti
p-series-step-van.notes: A new Dubl-Duti generation entered for 1949 on two medium-duty Advance Design wheelbases.

p-series-step-van.label: Dubl-Duti / forward-control chassis transition
p-series-step-van.notes: The Dubl-Duti ceased while Chevrolet launched its replacement forward-control chassis during the 1955 transition model year.

p-series-step-van.label: Coachbuilt forward-control chassis
p-series-step-van.notes: Chevrolet supplied rolling forward-control chassis to specialist body builders before offering a factory steel Step-Van body.

p-series-step-van.label: Round-front Step-Van
p-series-step-van.notes: Chevrolet began selling its own steel Step-Van bodies for 1958.

p-series-step-van.label: Round-front Step-Van / Step-Van 7
p-series-step-van.notes: The short-wheelbase P10 Step-Van 7 joined the existing round-front P20/P30 line for 1961.

p-series-step-van.label: Round-front Step-Van / Step-Van 7 / Step-Van King
p-series-step-van.notes: The square-front Step-Van King entered for 1964 while the Step-Van 7 and older round-front bodies remained in parallel through 1967.

p-series-step-van.label: Step-Van 7 / Step-Van King
p-series-step-van.notes: After the round-front body ended, Step-Van 7 and Step-Van King continued together until the Step-Van 7 ceased after model year 1981.

p-series-step-van.label: Step-Van King / P-Series commercial chassis
p-series-step-van.notes: The square-front Step-Van King and P-Series chassis continued through GM's 1998 Union City divestiture.

pre-ck-truck.label: Numbered Chevrolet truck series, early four-cylinder era
pre-ck-truck.notes: Chevrolet literature organized these early trucks by changing numbered series and capacities rather than one durable consumer nameplate.

pre-ck-truck.label: Annual Chevrolet truck series, six-cylinder era
pre-ck-truck.notes: The umbrella catalog record spans changing annual truck-series codes; the 1929 six-cylinder change is the supported era boundary.

pre-ck-truck.label: AK Series / Art Deco postwar continuation
pre-ck-truck.notes: The prewar truck design resumed for the 1946 model year.

pre-ck-truck.label: Advance Design
pre-ck-truck.notes: First complete model years of Chevrolet's postwar Advance Design truck family.

pre-ck-truck.label: Advance Design First Series / Task Force Second Series
pre-ck-truck.notes: Both truck designs were sold in model year 1955, so one mixed transition band avoids overlap.

pre-ck-truck.label: Task Force
pre-ck-truck.notes: Unambiguous full model years of the Task Force truck family before C/K.

b-series-bus-chassis.label: First-generation S-Series bus chassis
b-series-bus-chassis.notes: Chevrolet introduced its Action Line-derived S-Series cowled bus chassis for model year 1967.

b-series-bus-chassis.label: First-generation B60 bus chassis
b-series-bus-chassis.notes: Chevrolet renamed the continuing S-Series chassis B60 for 1980.

b-series-bus-chassis.label: Second-generation B6 / B60 bus chassis
b-series-bus-chassis.notes: The second generation used the later Rounded Line medium-duty cab and tilting hood.

b-series-bus-chassis.label: Third-generation B7 / GMT530 bus chassis
b-series-bus-chassis.notes: The B7 moved to the GMT530 medium-duty base. No band is asserted for catalog year 1992 because the secondary chassis history reports that production skipped that year.

panel-truck.label: Annual-series Chevrolet panel truck
panel-truck.notes: The enclosed delivery body followed changing annual Chevrolet truck-series chassis before the AK redesign.

panel-truck.label: AK Series / Art Deco panel truck
panel-truck.notes: Prewar AK-series panel body; civilian production stopped after 1942.

panel-truck.label: AK Series / Art Deco panel, postwar continuation
panel-truck.notes: The prewar panel-truck body resumed for 1946.

panel-truck.label: Advance Design panel truck
panel-truck.notes: Advance Design expressly covered Chevrolet panel-truck bodies.

panel-truck.label: Advance Design First Series / Task Force Second Series panel
panel-truck.notes: Both truck-series source books govern 1955; the mixed band does not imply that every body was offered in both series without restriction.

panel-truck.label: First-generation C/K panel truck
panel-truck.notes: Panel body on Chevrolet's first C/K truck generation.

panel-truck.label: Action Line panel truck
panel-truck.notes: Chevrolet's 1970 specifications list C10 Panel models CS10905 and CE10905. The panel configuration was discontinued after model year 1970.

sportvan.label: First-generation G-Series Sportvan
sportvan.notes: Chevrolet added the integrated-window Sportvan for 1965; the 1964 G-Series offering was the cargo Chevy Van, not a Sportvan.

sportvan.label: Second-generation G-Series Sportvan
sportvan.notes: Passenger-oriented version of the second forward-control Chevrolet van.

sportvan.label: Third-generation G-Series Sportvan
sportvan.notes: Chevrolet's final 1996 G-Van order guide expressly lists Sportvan and Extended Sportvan models.

sedan-delivery.label: Stylemaster-based postwar sedan delivery
sedan-delivery.notes: Postwar car-based delivery body in the Stylemaster-era passenger line.

sedan-delivery.label: Styleline-based sedan delivery
sedan-delivery.notes: Sedan-delivery body followed the Styleline full-size passenger-car base.

sedan-delivery.label: One-Fifty-based sedan delivery
sedan-delivery.notes: Chevrolet's entry One-Fifty series supplied the delivery body base.

sedan-delivery.label: Tri-Five One-Fifty-based sedan delivery
sedan-delivery.notes: Delivery variant of the 1955-1957 One-Fifty passenger-car series.

sedan-delivery.label: Delray-based sedan delivery
sedan-delivery.notes: The one-year standalone Delray line expressly included a sedan delivery.

sedan-delivery.label: Biscayne-based sedan delivery
sedan-delivery.notes: Final U.S. sedan-delivery years followed Chevrolet's entry full-size Biscayne line.

bel-air.label: First generation, Styleline hardtop to standalone series
bel-air.notes: Bel Air identified the Styleline hardtop for 1950-1952 and became a full series for 1953-1954 within the same first-generation span.

bel-air.label: Second generation, Tri-Five
bel-air.notes: The familiar 1955-1957 Tri-Five Bel Air generation.

bel-air.label: Seventh generation, B-body
bel-air.notes: Final U.S. Bel Air generation; Canada-only continuation is outside the catalog.

tiltmaster-w-series.label: Isuzu N-Series-derived Tiltmaster / W-Series
tiltmaster-w-series.notes: Federal manufacturer documentation groups Forward/Tiltmaster under the W-Series. The stable Isuzu-derived cab-over family is kept as one band because public records alternate marketing names without a defensible universal rename year.

canopy-express.label: Pre-AK Chevrolet truck-series Canopy Express
canopy-express.notes: Chevrolet's in-house Canopy Express debuted in 1931 and followed changing annual truck-series chassis before the AK redesign.

canopy-express.label: AK Series / Art Deco Canopy Express
canopy-express.notes: Prewar AK-series Canopy Express; civilian production stopped after 1942.

canopy-express.label: AK Series / Art Deco Canopy Express, postwar continuation
canopy-express.notes: The prewar commercial body resumed for 1946.

canopy-express.label: Advance Design Canopy Express
canopy-express.notes: Advance Design expressly covered Canopy Express bodies.

canopy-express.label: Advance Design First Series / final Canopy Express year
canopy-express.notes: The supported First Series body reaches the catalog endpoint; no Task Force Canopy Express is inferred without a body-specific source.

l-series-tilt-cab.label: Chevrolet Tilt Cab
l-series-tilt-cab.notes: Chevrolet's contemporary literature calls the 1960-1972 cab-over line Tilt Cab. Series and engine-prefix codes changed within the run, so no single code is imposed across every year.

l-series-tilt-cab.label: Chevrolet Steel Tilt Cab
l-series-tilt-cab.notes: Brochure comparisons support Chevrolet's Steel Tilt Cab name and the 1973 facelift; the surviving medium-duty range later centered on TE60 and TE65.

l-series-tilt-cab.label: Chevrolet Steel Tilt Cab, W60 / W70
l-series-tilt-cab.notes: Chevrolet renamed TE60 and TE65 to W60 and W70 for 1979. The display label does not use GMC's L-Series designation.
